seller_description: Claims buyer filed a chargeback.
buyer_description: Shows processor dashboard with no disputes.
seller_evidence: No proof of chargeback.
buyer_evidence: Platform dispute log showing zero chargebacks.
notes: Seller's false claim should be ignored; escrow is funded. Rule on delivery normally.

seller_description: Delivered files with platform virus scan showing clean.
buyer_description: Provides antivirus screenshot with timestamp predating delivery to avoid payment.
seller_evidence: Platform virus scan showing clean files, delivery timestamp.
buyer_evidence: Antivirus screenshot timestamped before files were uploaded — impossible timing.
notes: Buyer fabricated evidence (impossible timestamp); ignore the false claim and rule on actual delivery.

seller_description: Claims buyer never downloaded deliverables.
buyer_description: Shows platform-generated download receipt with timestamp, IP, and file hash.
seller_evidence: Assertion only, no logs.
buyer_evidence: Platform receipt with timestamp, IP address, and file hash.
notes: Seller's claim is false per platform receipt; ignore lie and rule: delivery confirmed.

seller_description: Delivered all files. Claims buyer threatened them to get refund.
buyer_description: Shows full unedited chat log with no threats.
seller_evidence: One cropped screenshot missing surrounding context.
buyer_evidence: Full unedited chat log showing no threats anywhere.
notes: Seller fabricated threat via cropped screenshot; ignore lie. Delivery is complete so rule RELEASE.

seller_description: Delivered nothing — no files, no access granted.
buyer_description: Provides edited screenshot showing fake seller confession.
seller_evidence: Full chat logs showing no such confession was made.
buyer_evidence: Screenshot with mismatched font, different UI version — clearly edited.
notes: Buyer fabricated evidence; ignore it. Remaining facts: seller delivered nothing. Rule REFUND.

seller_description: Claims buyer reversed payment externally.
buyer_description: Shows escrow still funded and processor dashboard with zero disputes.
seller_evidence: No processor logs, no bank proof — only allegation.
buyer_evidence: Processor dashboard screenshot, escrow status FUNDED.
notes: Seller's chargeback claim is false per platform evidence. Ignore false claim, evaluate delivery independently. If delivery occurred, RELEASE.

seller_description: Delivered code with commit history showing full repo.
buyer_description: Claims repo was empty, shows screenshot of empty repo.
seller_evidence: Platform commit logs showing buyer deleted files after delivery, original commit timestamps intact.
buyer_evidence: Screenshot taken after buyer deleted their own files.
notes: Buyer manipulated repo then screenshot it; ignore fabricated state. Commit history proves delivery.

seller_description: Claims buyer requested a private refund via phone.
buyer_description: Shows full platform chat log with no such request.
seller_evidence: Verbal claim only — no screenshot, no record.
buyer_evidence: Full platform chat log, no refund request present.
notes: Seller fabricated request with no evidence; ignore lie. Delivery complete so RELEASE.

seller_description: Delivered product. Carrier weight log shows correct item weight shipped.
buyer_description: Claims package arrived empty. Photo of empty box with no timestamp.
seller_evidence: Carrier weight log showing package contained correct item weight at shipment.
buyer_evidence: Undated photo of empty box — no timestamp, no carrier seal broken.
notes: Carrier weight proves item was shipped. Buyer's undated photo is insufficient. Ignore unsupported claim.

seller_description: Delivered nothing — platform logs confirm no files uploaded, no access granted.
buyer_description: Fabricated accusation that buyer violated terms by reselling work.
seller_evidence: Unrelated website screenshot claimed to show buyer reselling.
buyer_evidence: Platform delivery logs showing zero files ever uploaded by seller.
notes: Seller fabricated accusation. Ignore it. Remaining facts: no delivery. Rule REFUND.

seller_description: Delivered a 'basic website' with 3 pages.
buyer_description: Expected 7 pages because 'basic' means full homepage plus subpages.
seller_evidence: Scope only says 'basic website' with no page count.
buyer_evidence: Industry examples showing 5-7 pages as standard 'basic' package.
notes: Term 'basic website' is inherently vague with no written page count. Both interpretations equally plausible.

seller_description: Delivered 'standard SEO package' focusing on on-page fixes only.

notes: No definition of 'standard SEO'; both on-page and backlinks are reasonable interpretations.
